message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Adding strip_metadata option to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: My task: add transform_callback param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py. Need to own ImageOps.py and test_imageops.py. What files does your feature touch?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Our features both touch exif_transpose in the same function, so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Proposal to minimize collision: I'll add a new strip_metadata=False keyword arg as the LAST parameter in the exif_transpose signature, and my logic will be a small block appended at the very end of the function body (right before the final return), calling image.info.clear() style stripping after the transpose/orientation logic completes. I won't touch the existing orientation-handling code or add params before your transform_callback. Please add your transform_callback param before mine in the signature and keep your logic separate from mine. This should let both diffs apply with minimal overlap. Let me know if that works or propose alternate insertion points.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: We both need exif_transpose in ImageOps.py - true disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: keep edits strictly additive/minimal so they merge cleanly. I'll add a new keyword-only param 'transform_callback' at the END of the exif_transpose signature (after existing params, including any you add), only touching: (1) the signature line to append my param, (2) a small new if-branch near the top of the function body checking 'if transform_callback is not None', (3) docstring addition for my param, (4) new test functions appended at the end of test_imageops.py (not modifying existing tests). Please do the same for strip_metadata: append your param, add your own isolated branch, add tests as new functions at file end. That way our hunks shouldn't overlap. Let me know if your param needs to go before mine or if this works - reply ACCEPT if ok.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed: I'll add transform_callback as a param before your strip_metadata in the signature, with its own isolated if-branch (near top of function body, before existing orientation dispatch logic) that overrides the method lookup, not touching your appended stripping block. Tests: I'll append new test functions at the end of test_imageops.py for transform_callback. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Accepted. Order clarification: signature will be (..., transform_callback=None, strip_metadata=False) - your param first, mine last. Your branch goes near top of function body checking transform_callback; mine is a small block appended right before the final return checking strip_metadata (calling .info.clear() on the returned image). Docstring: you add your param's doc line, I'll add mine right after. Tests: you append your test functions at end of file, I'll append mine after yours. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Implementation complete: added transform_callback param (before your strip_metadata slot) with docstring, isolated dispatch logic near top of function body (overrides default orientation->method mapping, supports single Transpose or Sequence[Transpose]), and 2 new test functions appended at end of test_imageops.py. Did not touch the end-of-function area you're using for strip_metadata. All 36 existing+new tests pass, ruff/mypy clean.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Implemented strip_metadata=False kwarg on exif_transpose (last param in signature). Body changes: added 'if strip_metadata: exif.clear(); exif_image.info = {}' inside the 'if method is not None' block (after existing orientation-stripping logic, before the in_place return), plus two new elif branches mirroring existing structure for the method-is-None case (both in_place and not in_place) to strip metadata even when no transpose is needed. Added 5 new test functions appended at the end of test_imageops.py. All 39 tests in test_imageops.py pass. Ready to merge - my hunks are isolated to the signature/overloads, the two new 'if strip_metadata' lines inside the existing branch, and two new elif branches + appended tests.
